purpose: Central index of every Galerina signature SUITE: its id, algorithms, domain/context, status, and the signer + verifier bound to it. Mirror of the authoritative crypto-suite register (the source of truth); this file is kept reconciled to it so the runtime version-dispatch verifier and the conformance gate read one shared list.
ironRule: Retiring a suite means setting its status to verify-only-retired and KEEPING its verifier — never delete a verifier. A verifier verifies everything that suite ever signed; removing it makes historical artifacts fail admission.
envelopeScope: Three domain-separated signing families (governance-signature, audit-attestation, bridge-manifest), deliberately cross-protocol-separated so one ML-DSA key cannot be confused between surfaces. Version-dispatch happens WITHIN each domain, never across.

statusVocabulary.active-for-signing: production default; may sign new artifacts and verify existing ones
statusVocabulary.verify-only-retired: must NOT sign new artifacts; MUST keep verifying old ones (the iron rule)
statusVocabulary.open-decision: code-signable today but policy-undecided; treated as NOT signable until the owner rules, still verifiable
statusVocabulary.planned: documented but not implemented; no signer and no verifier — never signs or verifies

suites.note: Code-signable via signProofGraph and via the signProofGraphHybrid non-hybrid-key fallback, but NOT the production default. See securityFindings[signer-downgrade-fallback] and openDecisions[v1-retirement].
Certified/production default; the signing CLI pins this suite at multiple sites. Hybrid Ed25519 + ML-DSA-65.
pq_strict, ML-DSA-65 only. Documented in proof-graph.ts; no signer or verifier yet.

domains.note: Its own algorithm namespace — the signature.algorithm field carries Ed25519 / Ed25519+ML-DSA-65, NOT fungi.gov.sig.

suites.note: Stage 1 base. Which of Ed25519 vs the hybrid production emits has not been measured — see whatWasNotChecked.
Stage 2 (planned): ML-DSA (FIPS-204), SLH-DSA (FIPS-205). Verifier exists; hybrid signer not yet built.

domains.description: Bridge-manifest signing surface — signs the CF-3 BridgeManifest (deterministic-bridge admission).
domains.note: A distinct cross-protocol domain, implemented outside the core compiler. Verifiers fail closed with NO silent downgrade: verifyAttestationHybrid runs both halves (logical AND), and a requireHybrid / CRYPTO-002 policy DENIES a classical-only attestation — symmetric discipline, unlike the governance signer fallback.
domains.refactorPolicy: The implementing module was flagged possibly-frozen in an earlier note; its recent hardening (hybrid signing, requireHybrid) suggests that flag is stale, but part 2 MUST verify frozen-status before refactoring this domain's dispatch. If frozen, record the suite and handle read-only.

suites.note: Classical, backward-compatible default. A requireHybrid policy (tier-3 toxic-border admission) denies a classical-only attestation.
Hybrid, required for tier-3 toxic-border admission. verifyAttestationHybrid verifies both halves (logical AND, no PQ downgrade).

securityFindings.summary: signProofGraphHybrid silently downgrades to Ed25519 (fungi.gov.sig.v1) via signProofGraph when handed a non-hybrid key, or a key missing its ML-DSA private half. This is asymmetric with the verifier discipline: the verifiers reject a v2 signature missing its ML-DSA half (no PQ downgrade), but the signer will emit v1 without objection.
securityFindings.gateAction: The part-3 conformance gate should flag this fallback and, if v1 is retired, forbid new v1 signing.
securityFindings.livenessNote: Whether any production caller passes a non-hybrid key to signProofGraphHybrid has not been measured.

openDecisions.question: Should fungi.gov.sig.v1 become verify-only-retired — block new v1 signing, keep verifying old v1 artifacts, and fix the hybrid fallback to fail closed?
When to move fungi.gov.sig.v3 (pq_strict, ML-DSA-65 only) from planned to built — a suite-migration ceremony.
Whether to add SLH-DSA (FIPS-205) as a further audit-attestation suite.

whatWasNotChecked: Whether the bridge-manifest module is under an active code freeze — the possibly-frozen flag looks stale given its recent hybrid/requireHybrid hardening, but was not definitively confirmed lifted; part 2 verifies before refactoring.
Whether the v1 signing paths (signProofGraph and the hybrid fallback) are reachable from any production caller, or are dev/test/legacy only.
Which of the audit-attestation Ed25519 vs hybrid suites production emits today.
